dialogueChunks.intro: They know you're coming, {player_name}. They sent us a message.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: A dead crow nailed to the gate of {location} with a note: 'Send your hunter. We'll be waiting.' The {enemy_type} have fortified their position {location_hint} and they've been gathering reinforcements. {target_npc} says they spotted scouts on the ridgeline watching the settlement. They're not running: they're preparing for a fight. You'll need to hit them hard and fast before they finish their preparations.
dialogueChunks.outro: They think fear will stop us. Show them what justice looks like when it refuses to flinch.

playerResponses.text: Let them prepare. It won't save them.
Threats and fortifications? This just doubled in difficulty and price.
Walking into a trap isn't justice, it's stupidity.

dialogueChunks.intro: {player_name}, they've struck again. While you were hunting them, they circled back.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: The {enemy_type} raided {location} while most of the guards were out on patrol. They burned the mill and stole more {quest_item}. Nobody was killed this time, but {target_npc} was hurt trying to organize a defense. It's a message: they want us to know they can reach us whenever they want. We need to end this. Find their supply cache {location_hint} and destroy it. Without supplies, they'll have to move on or starve.
dialogueChunks.outro: They want to prove they're untouchable. Prove them wrong.

playerResponses.text: Burn their supplies? With pleasure. They started this.
So now I'm fighting a guerrilla war? This is escalating.
If they can hit the settlement that easily, nowhere is safe. I'm leaving.

dialogueChunks.intro: I've learned something that changes everything, {player_name}.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: {target_npc} captured one of the {enemy_type} scouts alive. Under questioning, the scout revealed that their leader isn't just a raider: they're a former soldier who deserted and has been building a warband for months. They have a forward camp {location_hint} with a stockpile of weapons and {quest_item} stolen from a dozen settlements. If we don't shut them down now, they'll grow into a force that no single settlement can resist.
dialogueChunks.outro: This is bigger than one crime. This is an army in the making. We need to cut the head off before it grows.

playerResponses.text: A deserter building a warband? That ends today.
An armed camp full of stolen weapons? You're asking a lot.
You need a militia, not one adventurer. This is too big for me.

dialogueChunks.intro: The plot thickens, {player_name}. Someone in {location} has been helping the enemy.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: {target_npc} found evidence that a trader in our own settlement has been feeding information to the {enemy_type}: patrol schedules, supply routes, when the storehouse is least guarded. That's how they've been staying one step ahead. The traitor fled when confronted, heading {location_hint} with a satchel of {quest_item} as payment for their betrayal. We need you to pursue them and bring back the evidence before they destroy it.
dialogueChunks.outro: Justice isn't just about punishing the enemy outside our walls. Sometimes the rot is closer to home.

playerResponses.text: A traitor? I'll bring them back and let the settlement decide their fate.
Chasing informants through the wild. This quest keeps getting more complicated.
Internal politics aren't my concern. Handle your own people.
